div.head:
No. 994.
Mr. Bayard to Mr. de Muruaga.
div.p: Sir: I have the honor to acknowledge the receipt of your note of the 10th instant, communicating information of measures to be taken by your Government in favor of the wine product, and especially inquiring if this Government would be disposed to participate in an international congress at Madrid to consider the general subject.

The proposal of the Government of Her Majesty the Queen Regent is one of great importance, upon which this Government would not wish to act except in accordance with the wishes of Congress, but before ascertaining the views of that body thereon, it is thought advisable to consult with the Commissioner of Agriculture, in whose especial charge the wine-growing interests of the United States are placed, to whom accordingly a copy of your note has been transmitted.
